section.name: Commons SCXML Security
section.p: In general, the Apache Commons Security Model specifies you should not assume Apache Commons methods to be safe to be used with untrusted input unless otherwise specified. This is especially true for Apache Commons SCXML: since the purpose of Commons SCXML is to execute code defined in SCXML scripts, we assume anyone who can provide SCXML scripts is to be fully trusted, including with the ability to execute arbitrary code.

This does not leave much attack surface: Commons SCXML is meant mainly for use in internal applications dealing with trusted input. If, after reviewing this model, you still believe you have found a possible vulnerability in Commons SCXML, please report this privately as documented on the Apache Commons Security page.
